message: --symbol combined with a lexical query filters exact symbol matches in Go AFTER the SQL candidate pool LIMIT (pool = max(limit*5, 50), ordered by raw bm25). Exact-name code_item hits that match the lexical query but rank below the pool cutoff are silently dropped and cannot be recovered by the re-rank. This violates the acceptance criterion that --symbol returns all matching results subject to --limit, and is inconsistent with the standalone --symbol path (querySymbol), which filters by title in SQL and finds every match. Fix: add the lower(title)=lower(?) predicate to the SQL WHERE clause when symbol is set so the pool only contains symbol matches.
evidence: internal/search/query.go:96-119 caps candidates at pool ordered by raw bm25 with kind forced to code_item; query.go:151 applies strings.EqualFold(result.Title, symbol) only to rows that survived the LIMIT. With default --limit 10 the pool is 50; >50 code_items lexically matching a common token (bm25 favors repeated tokens in name:/signature:/path: body lines, types.go:191-200) push exact-name hits past the cutoff. querySymbol (query.go:209-215) has no such gap, so the two --symbol forms return different result sets.

message: docs/flow.md's pactum search section (the prose doc enumerating search flags and ranking semantics) was not updated for this change: no mention of --symbol, the now-optional positional query, or the new path:start-end ranged addresses. The contract goal asks for the matching rule to be documented; it is documented only in CLI help text and executor guidance, leaving flow.md under-describing the command. Advisory doc drift, not an incorrect statement.
evidence: docs/flow.md:63-71 documents --kind values and bm25 ranking polish in detail but the diff touches no docs/ file except backlog.md; --symbol matching rule appears only in internal/app/cli.go:347 help text and the executor-context guidance in internal/app/prompt.go.

message: The new searchpkg.IsStaleIndex(err) branch in buildRunSearchResults (the upgrade scenario: map manifest ready but search.sqlite predates the v2 schema, surfacing ErrStaleIndex from runContextSearch during task new) has no test. TestSearchStaleSchemaPrintsGuidance covers only the App.Search CLI path, and TestRunContractOnlySearchResultsWarnWhenIndexMissing covers only the mapStatus.Status==stale early return at run.go:428. Reusing the existing DROP TABLE meta setup with `task new` would pin this branch.
evidence: grep for 'Search index is stale' shows only symbol_search_test.go:262 (App.Search path) and app_test.go:1020 (mapStatus-stale path, reached by deleting search.sqlite); no test reaches run.go:445-448 via a legacy-schema index with a ready map status.

message: The contract clause 'Return all duplicate exact symbol-name matches ... capped by --limit' is untested on both symbol paths: the SQL LIMIT in querySymbol (bare --symbol) and the post-filter truncation (query + --symbol). Duplicate-name tests (TestSymbolLookupReturnsExactCaseInsensitiveMatches, TestSymbolLookupReturnsDuplicateNamesWithDistinctRanges) use the default limit of 10 against 2 fixtures, so the cap is never exercised. A QueryOptions{Symbol: "Runner", Limit: 1} case would pin the cap and which duplicate wins under the deterministic order.
evidence: No test in internal/search/symbol_test.go or internal/app/symbol_search_test.go sets Limit or passes --limit alongside --symbol; querySymbol's LIMIT parameter and the lexical-path truncation after the symbol filter are both uncovered for the cap behavior.

message: ensureSchemaCurrent maps every error from the meta lookup to staleIndexError, not just the missing-table/missing-row cases it is designed for. Corruption, permission errors, and SQLITE_BUSY all surface to users as 'Search index is stale. Run: pactum map refresh' with exit code 0 (app.go:242-245, run.go:445-448), discarding the real error. The adjacent missing-index check in Query (query.go:43-48) distinguishes os.IsNotExist from other Stat errors and propagates the latter; the stale check should similarly treat only sql.ErrNoRows and the missing-meta-table error as stale and propagate the rest.
evidence: query.go:194-198 returns ErrStaleIndex(dbPath) on any Scan error; app.go Search prints refresh guidance and returns nil on IsStaleIndex, so the underlying error is never shown and the command exits 0. Mitigation: 'pactum map refresh' does recover corrupt files (Rebuild removes and recreates), so impact is a misleading diagnostic, not data loss — hence low/non-blocking.

message: User-visible search changes (--symbol flag, optional positional query, ranged path:start-end human output, new start_line/end_line/signature JSON fields) have no CHANGELOG.md Unreleased entry, although the changelog records exactly this class of change and every recent feature milestone PR updated it.
evidence: CHANGELOG.md Unreleased/Added records prior search-surface changes (--kind wiki/import at lines 15-16, ranking polish 17-19); git log shows M23.1/M24.1/M24.3 all updated it; this diff touches no changelog. New surface confirmed in internal/app/cli.go:344-347, internal/app/app.go:297-298, internal/search/types.go:90-92.

message: docs/flow.md's pactum search section (the prose doc that enumerates search flags) was not updated: it still shows the grammar as `pactum search "<query>"` with only --kind filtering, omitting --symbol, the now-optional positional query, and the ranged path:start-end + signature output for code_item hits.
evidence: docs/flow.md:63-71 enumerates --kind values and ranking but no --symbol; git log shows flow.md updated in nearly every recent milestone (M18.0-M24.2). The contract's 'document the matching rule' is met only in CLI help (internal/app/cli.go:347).

message: The committed agent skill package still teaches only the pre-slice search workflow: SKILL.md and references/workflow.md enumerate --kind filters and query styles but never mention --symbol or ranged first reads, although skill-driven external agents are the primary audience for symbol-addressed navigation and are not reached by the updated executor-context guidance in internal/app/prompt.go.
evidence: assets/agent-skills/pactum/SKILL.md:74-75 and references/workflow.md:36-42 list --kind filters only. Surface not named in contract scope, so deferral may be intentional; advisory.

message: `--symbol` filtering after a capped FTS candidate pool can drop valid exact symbol matches before they are considered.
evidence: The query limits to `pool` candidates before the exact `strings.EqualFold(result.Title, symbol)` filter, so exact matches ranked below that pool are omitted despite matching the contract.

message: Invalid-range code_item results can still emit `signature` in JSON output.
evidence: `hydrateSymbolMetadata` sets `result.Signature` even when the range is invalid; the JSON tag then emits `signature` without valid `start_line`/`end_line`.

message: Invalid-range metadata coverage asserts that signature is retained and does not test that --json omits symbol metadata for invalid code_item ranges.
evidence: The contract calls for invalid metadata omission coverage and JSON symbol metadata only for valid code_item metadata; this invalid-range test expects Signature to remain populated, while the JSON test covers only valid code_item metadata and non-code_item results.

message: Stale-index detection treats any metadata read error as a stale schema, hiding unrelated SQLite failures behind refresh guidance.
evidence: ensureSchemaCurrent maps every QueryRow/Scan error to ErrStaleIndex, and App.Search suppresses stale errors by printing 'Search index is stale. Run: pactum map refresh' and returning nil.

message: docs/flow.md still documents pactum search as query-only and only describes --kind; it does not document the new pactum search --symbol <name> lookup, optional positional query, or exact case-insensitive symbol matching.
evidence: docs/flow.md lines 63-71 describe only pactum search "<query>" and --kind, while internal/app/cli.go adds --symbol and makes the query optional when --symbol is given.
